mechanism_narrative: INDPRO has exhibited a consistent upward trend over the past 6 months, indicating expanding industrial production. This signals strong economic activity that drives corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If walk-forward Rank IC <= 0 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months during the signal period.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate is at zero lower bound (0.15%), consistent with an accommodative monetary policy stance. As long as FEDFUNDS remains at or below 0.25% for at least 2 consecutive months, equity valuations are supported by low discount rates, predicting positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% and positive forward returns still occur in more than 60% of triggered instances, or if negative returns occur in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) increases by more than 200k month-over-month and retail sales (RSAFS) also increase month-over-month above 0.5%, it confirms robust labor market and consumer demand, which boosts corporate profits and stock returns over the next 20 days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly increase > 200k AND RSAFS monthly increase > 0.5%
failure_condition: If in months meeting the joint condition, forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive at the 10% level by one-sided t-test over the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are currently at a depressed level of 510k. In a post-recession recovery regime, a crossing of HOUST above 600k signals a meaningful improvement in the housing sector, which spills over to consumer durables and construction, driving equity prices higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If after HOUST crosses above 600k, forward 60-day returns are negative in more than one-third of instances, or the signal does not produce a positive mean excess return over a null model.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE decrease of at least 0.2 percentage points) provides a rapid signal of labor market strengthening, which immediately improves investor sentiment and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If in months meeting the trigger, 5-day forward returns are positive less than 60% of the time, or the average return is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) have been trending upward, indicating strong consumer demand. A sustained upward slope over 3 months is a reliable indicator of economic momentum, which translates into positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 3-month moving average of RSAFS declines month-over-month, or forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of overlapping periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero federal funds rate and rising retail sales creates a powerful macro environment: accommodative monetary policy supports asset prices while consumer spending growth validates the earnings outlook. Jointly, these conditions predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% and RSAFS > previous month value
failure_condition: If the joint condition produces forward 20-day returns with a negative mean over the walk-forward period, or if the signal is not statistically significant at the 5% level using a one-sided test.

mechanism_narrative: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth above 2.5% signals strong demand expansion. Given the latest GDPC1 reading of 11340.9 indicates positive growth, this sustained demand momentum supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the next quarterly GDP growth rate is below 2.0% annualized or if forward 60-day returns after the trigger are negative in more than 40% of occurrences, the hypothesis is falsified.
